content_markdown: A particle moving in a circle completes 5 revolutions in 3 s. What is the frequency?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: Frequency is defined as the number of cycles (or revolutions) per unit time. To find the frequency $f$, you divide the total number of revolutions by the total time taken: $f = \frac{\text{revolutions}}{\text{time}} = \frac{5}{3} \text{ Hz}$.
